Whether you're selling on Amazon, Noon, or other regional marketplaces, understanding your true profitability is essential. This episode explores the challenges that impact margins, common mistakes businesses make, and practical ways to optimize operations while scaling across Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and the wider GCC region. Tune in to discover:
  • The current state of GCC eCommerce marketplaces
  • Why many brands struggle with profit despite growing sales
  • Hidden costs and margin leakage that reduce profitability
  • Strategies to improve operational efficiency and maximize margins
  • Best practices for scaling across the GCC market
  • Future trends shaping eCommerce in the Middle East
If you're an eCommerce entrepreneur, marketplace seller, brand owner, or digital commerce professional, this episode provides valuable insights to help you grow smarter and build a more profitable business in the GCC.

🎙️ Subscribe to Let's Talk eCommerce (LTE) for expert discussions on eCommerce, digital transformation, AI, Shopify, marketplace strategies, and business growth across the GCC and beyond.

If you've ever lost thirty minutes wondering about something completely ri

title: Aging Parent Podcast
author: Valerie Upham
description: If you're raising kids, building a career, and suddenly managing doctor appointments, finances, or hard conversations with your parents, this podcast is for you. I'm Valerie Upham, and this is The Aging Parent Podcast. This show is for the sandwich generation, the sons and daughters who didn't plan on becoming caregivers, but stepped into the role anyway. You're carrying responsibility, love, pressure, and decisions that matter more than anyone prepared you for. I know this season personally. I've supported a parent through dementia. I've walked alongside my father through lung cancer, and both through hospice. I've lived in the tension between relationships and responsibilities. Caring for your parents doesn't come with a roadmap. Even with the best intentions, the choices can feel heavy because they affect the people that you love most. You may not know which decisions need to happen first. You may be feeling pulled between your parents' wishes and living your own life. You may be navigating siblings and doctors without a clear plan, quietly wondering if you're honoring your parents the right way while trying to protect the relationship. What you want is steadiness, clarity instead of second-guessing, confidence instead of guilt, a way forward that supports your parents without losing yourself. Each week, you'll hear solo episodes and thoughtful interviews with estate planning and elder law attorneys, financial planners, memory care leaders, healthcare professionals, and people who have walked this road themselves. You'll gain clarity on what needs attention and when. You'll learn language for conversations that preserve dignity and trust. You'll understand care and housing options before urgency forces decisions, and you'll be reminded that uncertainty does not mean that you're doing it wrong. New episodes will drop weekly, so whenever new questions surface, you have a place to return. Share this with a sibling or a friend who needs it, and join me on The Aging Parent Podcast!

In a world where winning is everything and everyone is watching, Yeardley sits down with professional athletes to find out how they personally measure success, and who they are when the uniform comes off.

These are warm, wide-ranging conversations with professional athletes about the careers that shaped them, the lives they’ve built beyond their sport, and the experiences, passions, quirks, and questions that make them who they are. Then, before time runs out, each guest faces a rapid-fire round inspired by the clock in their game.

Every player, in every sport, in every position, has a story. Guard the Yard wants to hear it.

Brought to you by Audio 99, the award-winning team behind the hit true crime podcast Small Town Dicks.

title: Global Mystery Archive: The Fringe Network
author: Global Mystery Archive
description:
UFO sightings, government cover-ups, and phenomena that sit right at the edge of what science can explain. The Fringe Network investigates paranormal cases and conspiracy theories with a sci-fi-tinged curiosity — asking the questions officials would rather leave alone.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: First We Read: The Odyssey
author: First We Read
description: Raghu Karnad and Supriya Nair read and talk through two modern English translations of Homer's Odyssey. Edited and produced by Saachi.

title: Vision & Vows
author: Angel & Kayla Mendoza
description: Vision & Vows is where Angel and Kayla Mendoza pull up two chairs and talk honestly about building a life and a business on faith. Real marriage, real family, real deals. Each week they share what it actually looks like to raise three boys, grow a real estate portfolio in Amarillo, Texas, and keep God at the center of all of it. Expect encouragement, hard-won lessons, and practical wisdom you can carry into your own home, marriage, and work. If you want to chase big vision without losing sight of your vows, you belong here.

title: A World Of Trouble
author: Voyage Media
description: A young boy's world of imagination suddenly seems to be no longer in his imagination. Starring David Errigo, the voice of "Ferb" in Phineas and Ferb. From Voyage Media, producer of the hit children's podcast Defenders of Monstrovia.

title: Idols Aside Podcast
author: Idols Aside Ministries
description: Bringing hope to the fatherless through the heart and life changing gospel of Jesus Christ.

author: Dr. Bones

description: Welcome to the B-side where two best friends talk B-sides of music and life all in one place. Don't forget to turn the record over.

title: 𝑻𝒉𝒆 𝑷𝒉𝒊𝒍² 𝑺𝒆𝒔𝒔𝒊𝒐𝒏𝒔 - Marketing for Financial Advice Professionals
author: Phil Calvert and Phil Bray
description:
The marketing and lead generation podcast for financial planners, IFAs and financial advice professionals - with Phil Bray of The Yardstick Agency and Phil Calvert of LifeTalk.
In each episode, the two Phils take a relaxed, conversational look at what's happening in financial adviser marketing - what's working, what's changing and what's worth paying attention to. Drawing on decades of experience, they share ideas, observations, examples and practical insights from across the profession.
There's no script and no corporate spin - just honest discussion about marketing for advice businesses.

title: Vital Horizon
author: Vital Horizon
description: Vital Horizon is your trusted health and wellness podcast, bringing expert insights on nutrition, fitness, mental well-being, longevity, and healthy living. Each episode delivers practical, science-backed advice to help you build healthier habits and live your best life.

title: The Grief Portal
author: Dani Hickman
description:
The Grief Portal is a podcast about learning how to tend to grief—not as something to overcome or move beyond, but as an inevitable companion to a life touched by love.
Hosted by trauma psychotherapist, author and entrepreneur Dani Hickman, the show explores what it means to create more spaciousness in the body and in our lives to be present with grief. Through conversations, lived stories, practices, and reflections, The Grief Portal offers new ways of engaging with grief, widening our capacity to hold what is here, and becoming students of our own grief.
All gates of grief belong here. At the heart of the podcast is the belief that grief is not meant to be carried alone. Together, we'll explore how tending to our grief can deepen our capacity for connection, compassion, wonder, and aliveness—and, when the timing is right, begin to uncover the medicine our grief may hold.

title: Rainbow Dragons
author: Butter Frost
description: Hey clawmates, this is Rainbow Dragons, a Wings of Fire podcast. We will be talking about my fan fics, OCs and how to make them, funny and happy moments in the books, Wings of Fire character ratings and so much more! Please listen to my podcast. I am your host Butterfrost. I hope you enjoy!

title: Authony's Radio
author: Steven Authony
description: The home of Steven Authony's thoughts and real experiences. Mondays @ 6am.

title: Heavy Victory
author: KSE Podcasts
description: What is it about high achievers accomplishing more than most will dare, and those who overcome obstacles that many would find too much to bear? For Sergeant Justin Dodge, a leader in Denver's METRO/SWAT unit, who lost his leg during the Denver Nuggets Championship parade yet returned to fully operational status in under a year, the answer is the active pursuit of wellness. Join him as he shares his 29 years of experience with the Denver Police Department, and two decades with the city's elite, tier one, full-time tactical unit, to explore journeys through wellness, this is Heavy Victory.

title: Ministry with Coffee
author: Tricia Bullard
description: Ministry with Coffee Podcast is a place for authentic conversations rooted in biblical truth and real-life faith. Hosted by Tricia Bullard, each episode features pastors, authors, ministry leaders, and believers sharing stories of God's faithfulness, practical wisdom, and encouragement for everyday life. Whether you're seeking hope, spiritual growth, or a deeper walk with Jesus, pull up a chair, grab a cup of coffee, and join the conversation.

title: Unrooted, a Rooted & Rising Podcast
author: Yadhira Cordero
description: Unrooted is the raw, unfiltered, BTS conversation behind building something that lasts. Hosted by Yadhira Cordero, we talk about the real stuff, the mindset shifts, identity crises, business lessons, and faith moments nobody puts in their highlight reel. Each episode pulls back the curtain on what it actually takes to build a life you love: the beliefs you have to unlearn, the people you have to release, and the daily reps that make the difference between who you are and who you're becoming. If you've ever felt like you were in between versions of yourself, this podcast was made for you.

title: The HOBY Podcast
author: Casey Casteel
description: The HOBY Podcast shares authentic, personal stories that illustrate the impact of HOBY through the lived experiences of alumni, volunteers, stakeholders and supporters. Follow along as we build understanding, strengthen connection, and inspire impact.

title: Nua 2026
author: Nua
description: NUA Festival 2025. A festival where friends from different churches, networks and communities have committed to come together with a unified desire to seek God for a fresh outpouring of his Holy Spirit on the island of Ireland.

title: Say it to my Face . . . Podcast
author: MaryAnna
description:
The internet didn't invent mobs, gossip, outrage, or public shaming. It just made them faster.
Join writer and researcher MaryAnna Clemons as she explores the history, psychology, and social dynamics behind human behavior—from Roman curse tablets and Pompeii graffiti to TikTok pile-ons, online harassment, and modern internet outrage.
Because the technology changes. The people don't.
See you online.

description: Interviews with authors of Notre Dame Press books.

author: NOPI
description: Uncovering the Unknown

title: Big Law: Legal Myths Exposed
author: Big Law
description:
Big Law: Legal Myths Exposed is the podcast that separates legal fact from fiction.


Every day, Queenslanders make important decisions based on advice they've heard from friends, family, social media—or worse, common myths. 


Hosted by the team at Big Law, each episode unpacks one widespread legal misconception and explains what the law actually says in clear, practical terms.


From property and conveyancing to wills, business, family law and beyond, our experienced lawyers break down complex legal topics into straightforward conversations that help you make informed decisions and avoid costly mistakes.


Because when it comes to the law, what you don't know can cost you in more ways than one.

This is my very interesting podcast

title: JMBD Podcast
author: John M.B. Davis
description:
Hosted by John M.B. Davis, each episode features thoughtful, accessible Bible studies designed to help you understand God more clearly, examine yourself honestly, and practice your faith intentionally. Through biblical depth, cultural insight, and practical wisdom, we explore what Scripture reveals about God, identity, healing, relationships, leadership, and everyday discipleship.
Join our Patreon Community (https://www.patreon.com/cw/johnmbdavis)
Visit our Website (https://www.johnmbdavis.com)

title: Midnight Rentals
author: Celeste and Autumn
description:
Two best friends. Minimal research. Absolutely insane movies. 
Join Autumn and Celeste as they dive headfirst into the glorious dumpster fire of cinema and pop culture. Why do people like these things so much? Why do we like it that much?! What’s wrong with all of us!!?!
Between the low budgets, questionable acting, and plots that defy all logic, they somehow find time to spill on pop culture, overshare about their lives, and debate things that have absolutely nothing to do with the film. New episodes every month(at least). Snacks encouraged.

title: TheJordivanZanenShow
author: Jordi van Zanen
description: Amateur Australian comedian rants about current events while having political and philosophical takes.

title: The Sports Huddle with Chris Kelly
author: Chris Kelly
description:
The Sports Huddle is Western North Carolina's home for local sports conversation, analysis, and storytelling. Hosted by your ringmaster Chris Kelly, the show airs mostly on Thursdays at 6–8 PM on Bigfoot Country WBRM/WZGM, bringing listeners the latest on high school athletics, college sports, and professional teams across the region and beyond.

With multiple years of play-by-play experience and deep roots in the local sports community, Chris delivers insightful interviews, game breakdowns, coaching perspectives, and lively discussion that keeps fans connected to the athletes, teams, and stories that matter most. Whether it's Friday night football, championship basketball, recruiting news, or the biggest headlines from around the sports world, The Sports Huddle is where Western North Carolina sports fans gather each week to talk the game.

Tune in live on Bigfoot Country or catch the podcast anytime to stay informed, engaged, and connected to the Carolina sports scene.

title: Inside Access
author: Inside Access
description:
Welcome to Inside Access, where we get real about building wealth. This show is for everyday Australians who want clarity around property investing and a plan that actually fits their life. We work with homeowners, existing investors, and anyone who knows their path to wealth isn’t going to be achieved through their 9-5. 
Hosted by Dory Senior and Ben Senior, every week, we unpack how strategic property investing really works, from understanding your borrowing capacity to structuring your finance, researching the right locations, and making informed decisions.
Stay tuned for no BS information on how to properly invest in property based on your circumstances.

title: Burning Thoughts
author: Burning Thoughts
description: Die-hard Flames fans Derek and Liam bring you honest, unfiltered takes on Calgary Flames hockey. The good, the bad, and the burning thoughts in between.

title: SURRENDER with Ronnie Hendricks
author: Dead End Kulture
description: SURRENDER with Ronnie Hendricks explores the moments that change our lives. Through honest conversations with people from all walks of life, each episode uncovers how letting go of fear, pride, control, or the past can open the door to growth, purpose, and lasting transformation.

title: Sex and Psychology Podcast
author: Dr. Justin Lehmiller
description: The Sex and Psychology Podcast is the sex ed you never got in school—and won’t find anywhere else. Kinsey Institute researcher Dr. Justin Lehmiller takes you on a journey through the psychology of sex and relationships, offering practical tips along the way that can help you take your intimate life to the next level. Learn more on Dr. Lehmiller’s blog at sexandpsychology.com

title: The Best Podcast in the World
author: Allison Barnett
description:
Congratulations, you've just found the best podcast in the world.


We're two best friends without a plan but a lot of heart. Each week we'll talk about something for an hour, or maybe longer, or maybe not a full hour. But we'll talk, you'll laugh and we'll all cry. Welcome home.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Brilliant Tribe Podcast
author: The Brilliant Tribe
description: A podcast that encourages believers to reflect the brilliance of heaven here on earth.

title: Zero to Agent in 30 Minutes
author: O'Reilly
description:
Go from zero to a fully working AI agent in just half an hour, every single week.
Join us for this weekly build series where you’ll learn to create real production-ready agents live. Each 30-minute sprint focuses on building a different working agent while introducing the latest AI tools, powerful techniques, pro tips, and clever tricks.
We don’t just show you what agents can do. We show you how they think. You’ll learn the specific skills required to manage memory, integrate external tools, and orchestrate multi-agent swarms.

title: The Profitable Therapist
author: Hannah Ciampini, LCSW
description:
You didn't become a therapist to spend your evenings writing notes, stressing over cancellations, or wondering if seeing more clients is the only way to earn a good living.


Welcome to The Profitable Therapist, the podcast for therapists who want to build a profitable private practice that supports their life...not one that runs it.


Hosted by therapist and business coach Hannah Ciampini, each short, actionable episode shares practical strategies to help you grow your income, attract your ideal clients, market your practice with confidence, and build a business that doesn't depend on packing your calendar with weekly sessions.

From pricing and premium offers to SEO, AI, websites, systems, consult calls, and sustainable practice growth, you'll learn real-world strategies you can implement right away—without compromising your ethics or the quality of care you provide.


If you're ready to stop believing that more clients are the only path to more income and start building a private practice that's both profitable and fulfilling, you're in the right place.


Because there is more than one way to be a therapist.

title: Kenneth Corbin Podcast
author: Kenneth Corbin Podcast
description: Teaching and preaching bible

title: Hick and The Yank
author: Briley Boys
description: Guys being dudes. Talking sports.

title: Priddy Podcast
author: Mariah Priddy
description:
Welcome to The Priddy Podcast - A dedicated space for honest, unfiltered conversations at the intersection of authenticity, self-expression, and personal evolution. Hosted by Mariah Priddy, this show explores what it truly means to live boldly, embrace your duality, and step fully into your own presence.

From deep dives into holistic wellness, femininity, and sensuality to navigating judgment, identity shifts, and the quiet spaces in between, The Priddy Podcast is an invitation to strip away expectations, cultivate radical self-acceptance, and create a safe environment to simply be. Consider this your permission slip to take up space and live in your full spectrum.

title: The Well-Read Life Book Club
author: Tanya Anderson
description:
What books curate a well-read life? 

My book club focuses on fiction, nonfiction, even children's literature, to enhance one's life with story and thought.

In addition, I pick a title every month to read and reflect; join me!

title: The Daily Spark Podcast
author: echoahickskhoksa2
description:
Welcome to The Daily Spark , your daily source for everything happening in the world of artificial intelligence.

From the latest  news and breakthrough technologies to practical tools, productivity tips, and real-world applications, each episode delivers clear, engaging, and easy-to-understand insights. Whether you're a student, creator, entrepreneur, or simply curious about the future, this podcast helps you stay informed and inspired.

Join us as we explore how AI is transforming business, education, creativity, and everyday life—one spark at a time.

New episodes every week.

title: Witches, Bitches, and Sluts
author: Xtina Rose
description:
Witch. Bitch. Slut. These are just a sample of the many names women have been called throughout history through today.


Witches, Bitches, and Sluts is a twice-weekly show about how women get discredited through these labels, and why the false version so often outlives the real one. Every Monday, host Xtina Rose takes on one woman's story — the accusation, what actually happened, and why the myth won anyway. Every Thursday, a Rabbit Hole episode goes deeper into the research: the psychology, the history, and the patterns that show up again and again, across centuries and headlines, whenever a woman gets reduced to one word.


Rigorously researched, casually told — this isn't a lecture, it's the conversation you'd have with a friend who did way too much reading before showing up.

title: Soul to Earth podcast
author: Carey Kaas
description: Welcome to the "Soul to Earth" podcast. I am Carey Kaas, a Psychic, Evidential Medium, Liminal Witch, and Mentor. My intention with this podcast is to support and inspire you on your spiritual path. Sharing topics ranging from trusting your Intuition and connecting with your Guides and loved ones in Spirit to exploring Modern Witchcraft, the Akashic Records, working with Deities, energy healing, and past lives to expand your awareness in a grounded and meaningful way, making the Supernatural more natural.

title: Beyond the Report - AI in PI Law Firms
author: Yuval Goren
description: A practical podcast series with Yuval Goren on how personal injury law firms can adopt AI safely, st

title: The Saientific Podcast

title: Leadership with Sonia
author: Sonia McDonald
description: Leadership isn't a title — it's how you show up.  Welcome to Leadership with Sonia. Real conversations. Courageous leadership.  I'm Sonia McDonald — CEO and Founder of LeadershipHQ, keynote speaker, executive coach, and author of First Comes Courage, Leadership Attitude and Just Rock It! For more than 35 years I've worked with leaders and teams across Australia and beyond, helping them lead with courage, kindness and impact.  This show is where leadership gets real. No fluff. No jargon. No corporate speak. Just honest conversations about what it actually takes to lead — backed by neuroscience and decades of experience in the trenches.  What you'll get every episode:  🎙️ Real talk on courageous leadership — including the conversations most leaders avoid 🧠 The brain science behind great leadership, explained simply 💛 Practical tools you can use the same day — at work, at home, in life 🔥 Stories from the front line of leadership — the wins, the failures and the lessons 🚀 Interviews with leaders who lead with heart and back themselves  Whether you're a CEO, an emerging leader, or someone rebuilding your career from scratch (I've been there — 40 job rejections before I built my own company), this podcast is for you.  Because the world needs great leadership — and it starts with you.  New episodes weekly. Subscribe so you never miss one.  Connect with Sonia: 🌐 Website: https://www.soniamcdonald.com.au 🏢 LeadershipHQ: https://www.leadershiphq.com.au 💼 LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/soniamcdonald 📚 Books: First Comes Courage | Leadership Attitude | Just Rock It! ✉️ Newsletter: sign up at leadershiphq.com.au  Work with Sonia: Keynote speaking | Executive coaching | Leadership programs | Courageous Conversations workshops  #Leadership #CourageousLeadership #LeadershipDevelopment #ExecutiveCoaching #Neuroscience #WomenInLeadership #LeadershipPodcast

title: Airplane Therapy
author: Shloime Zionce
description: The world is bigger, kinder, and more fascinating than the headlines suggest. Airplane Therapy is a podcast about the people we meet, the places we discover, and the conversations that stay with us long after we land.

title: THE JAMBALAYA BLOODHOUND BAWCAST
author: Jambalaya Bloodhound
description: Jambalaya Bloodhound is a South Louisiana comedy swamp pop band that makes super niche songs about Cajun country and Acadiana. In The Bawcast, Marc, Ty, and Coleman meet weekly to discuss old and new Cajun/South Louisiana stuff.

title: The Cocktail Hour Podcast
author: Camiloevilla10
description: Drinks got cancelled? Don't worry! Get your cocktail and immerse yourself in this new podcast where we explore identity, growth, and relationships while pouring it all out. Chin chin! Cheers! Salud!

title: My 24/7
author: Brian Ng, Carl Chandler
description: My 24/7 is a show about what happens when you leave the safety of a tech career to build something of your own. Hosts Brian Ng and Carl share the unfiltered lessons — money, mindset, mistakes — from making the leap into entrepreneurship, one week at a time.

title: Navigating Between The Lines
author: Maggie Feil
description: Daily conversations brought into episodes to validate the human experience by blending honest storytelling, humor and healing. Empowering women through life's transitions.

title: Double Life
author: Podcast Studios
description:
Double Life

Some people hide an affair. Others hide an entire life. Double Life is an original true story podcast about the hidden lives of the people we trusted most—parents, children, spouses, siblings, friends, neighbors, mentors, business partners, and the people who quietly shaped our lives while keeping devastating secrets.

Every episode begins with one small crack in reality: a strange conversation, a missing document, a chance encounter, a detail that doesn't quite fit. What follows is the emotional unraveling of trust as ordinary people discover that someone they loved was living a completely different life.

These are stories about betrayal, deception, identity, and the devastating moment when everything you believed about someone changes forever.

The lies we don't see.
The hidden lives of those we trusted.

An original production from SOFI Studios.

title: Watch the Dark
author: Michael
description:
Atmospheric horror stories from the edge of the firelight.

Original horror fiction rooted in dread, wilderness, isolation, and the things that wait beyond the tree line.

title: Unpeeled by VI PEEL
author: Marya
description: Unpeeled is the official VI Peel podcast, where we peel back the science, strategy, and stories behind aesthetic medicine. Each episode features expert insights on skincare, ingredients, clinical education, business growth, and the trends shaping our industry—all designed to help practitioners and our team learn, grow, and stay ahead.

title: in time with God
author: kcrock
description: Every month is a chance to hear from the Lord in time and keep moving forward with Him, gaining insight and understanding that leads to breakthrough and new revelations so you can keep progressing in your walk with the Lord. You’ll gain both historical and biblical understanding through simple teachings as well as prophetic insight to help guide you through each month. Following the biblical calendar has led me to live intentionally with Jesus like never before. I’d love for you to journey with me as insight develops and God’s spirit leads us through each new month!

title: BRAND NEW
author: Paul Glass
description:
A 21 Day Introduction to Faith - Audio Devotional.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: MotherLoad
author: CHEK Podcasts
description: MotherLoad is a space for honest conversations about the invisible load women carry: motherhood, wellness, resilience, identity, and everything in between. Because strength isn't about perfection; it's about rebuilding, reconnecting, and becoming who we're meant to be through every season of life.

title: K-haos C-coordinators
author: Kelly and Carrie
description:
Welcome to the podcast where you truly never know what’s going to happen next!

We’re Kelly and Carrie, and we work in a school supporting kids with autism and emotional support needs—and trust us when we say, we couldn’t make these stories up if we tried.

From getting hit, kicked, every bodily fluid thrown at us, and called every name in the book… to kids drinking urinal water—yes, you heard that right—to the hilarious moments that leave us crying with laughter and the unforgettable memories that remind us exactly why we love what we do.

Our jobs can be chaotic, challenging, exhausting, hilarious, rewarding, and completely unpredictable—sometimes all before lunch!

So buckle up, because we’re sharing our unfiltered thoughts, unbelievable stories, experiences, laughs, and everything in between.

This is life behind the classroom doors—and trust us, it’s never boring!

title: Atown's Podcast Show
author: ANTHONY F WIDEMAN
description: Atown interviews Everyday people doing extraordinary things in life. His stutter adds a comedic relief to some of their heart wrenching moments. Listen Up & Enjoy!

title: The Tension Lab
author: Wrenterprise Solutions
description:
Most high performers were never taught how they respond when life turns up the heat. They just react and spend years managing the aftermath.
The Tension Lab is a podcast for professionals ready to understand what's running underneath their performance, relationships, and health when pressure builds and demands collide.
Each episode explores the patterns, signals, and costs of unmanaged tension delivering practical tools for building the self-awareness and regulation skills that high achievement never required you to develop. Until now.

title: Life is but a Dream Podcast
author: Becky Dreps & Liv Toepfer
description:
Life Is But a Dream is a podcast exploring the dream nature of reality through astrology, mysticism, psychology, quantum thought, and the unseen architecture of consciousness.
This isn’t just a podcast about dreams. It’s a call to work with them consciously.
Our deepest desire with Life Is But a Dream is to inspire you to engage in daily dream work…not as fantasy, but as a living, creative, consciousness-expanding practice.

title: The NorCal Football Guys
author: KG & Tex
description: Unbiased coverage of high school football in California's Northern Section.

title: Witchcraft to worship
author: Dalton & Natalie
description: Witchcraft to Worship is the true story of Dalton and Natalie, a journey from suicide, witchcraft, addiction, and brokenness to freedom in Jesus Christ. Through raw testimony and honest conversations about spiritual warfare, deliverance, healing, marriage restoration, and the gospel, they share how Jesus transformed their lives. No topic is off limits.

title: Tikiman and the Viking
author: Brian Johnson, HB Berlow
description: Two writers and creatives in the Midwest trying to find their muse among the wheat and where we talk to other creatives about their process.

title: Slapshots and Soundwaves: Hockey Talk and Hot Takes
author: Jasbir Singh Randhawa
description: Hosted by Jeff Randhawa Slapshots & Soundwaves takes an in-depth look at everything Edmonton Oilers—from game breakdowns and roster moves to playoff races and the biggest storylines shaping the season. With passionate analysis, honest opinions, and a true fan’s perspective, Jeff Randhawa brings Oil Country together for engaging hockey talk both on and off the ice.

title: Her Nature: For Girls Who GTFO
author: Madison Ford
description: Her Nature is a raw, honest mix of stories, reflections, and real-life tools for women who do it all. It’s a no bullshit conversation you’d have with a friend over a long voice note or on the trail. Mixing ambition with a little breathing room, we discuss honoring both your dreams and your needs without ditching your hot girl energy. Growth, cycles, health, and happiness- making it work your way, because it’s in your nature.

title: The HoaQ Podcast
author: HoaQ Club
description:
HoaQ Club is a community of creators, operators, and investors backing entrepreneurs building scalable businesses for Africa and its diaspora.

Through our monthly AMA sessions, we bring together founders, investors, and ecosystem leaders to share practical insights, discuss the opportunities and challenges shaping Africa's technology ecosystem, and help our community learn from those building at the forefront.

Whether you're a founder, operator, investor, or simply curious about the future of technology in Africa, this podcast is your front-row seat to the conversations shaping the ecosystem.

Visit HoaQ.co to join our community, and follow us across social media @HoaQClub.

title: The Third Space
author: Colleen
description:
The Third Space is a podcast by Trinity Vitality exploring the stories, beliefs, and experiences that shape what it means to be human.
A “third space” is a place beyond our everyday roles and expectations, where we can connect, reflect, and explore deeper questions of life, healing, spirituality, and meaning.
Through conversations with people from diverse backgrounds and worldviews, we create space for curiosity, compassion, and understanding. This is a place where psychology, spirituality, and human stories meet.

title: The Steelman
author: A4S Podcast Studios
description: The Steelman is a judged debate podcast that stress-tests today's most contested ideas through lively, disciplined disagreement. In every episode, the Defender of Unpopular Ideas takes on a worthy challenger before a judge challenges both sides and delivers a final verdict.

title: The Word on Wall Street
author: Kevin Bruce CFA, CFP,® CKA®
description: The Word on Wall Street is your daily one-minute market recap with a biblical perspective.

title: The Rooted Mother - Where faith meets everyday motherhood.
author: Michelle
description:
For years, I battled anxiety, postpartum depression, panic attacks, and the overwhelming weight of believing I wasn't enough as a mom. I loved my children deeply, but there were days I felt like I was simply surviving.


Everything began to change when I stopped trying to carry motherhood on my own and started rooting my life in Christ.


I'm Michelle, a wife, homeschooling mom of four, former public school teacher, military spouse, and the founder of Rooted & Thriving. Through every move, every season of loneliness, every anxious thought, and every hard day of motherhood, God has faithfully met me with His peace. Now my heart is to help other moms experience that same hope.


Welcome to The Rooted Mother - a Christian motherhood podcast for overwhelmed moms who want to trade anxiety, mom guilt, and survival mode for biblical peace, lasting joy, and a faith that's deeply rooted in Jesus.


Each week, we'll have honest conversations about motherhood, anxiety, postpartum healing, marriage, faith, biblical encouragement, spiritual growth, and the everyday challenges of raising a family. You'll leave every episode feeling encouraged, equipped, and reminded that you don't have to walk this journey alone.


Whether you're in the newborn stage, chasing toddlers, homeschooling your kids, or simply longing to become the mother God created you to be, this podcast is here to help you find peace in Christ, one conversation at a time.


🌿 Start here: Download my free printable, God's Truth vs. Mom Guilt, and learn to replace guilt-filled thoughts with God's truth and Scripture:
https://www.rootedandthrivingmama.com/gods-truth-vs-mom-guilt


Next Steps

🌿 Website & Shop:
https://www.rootedandthrivingmama.com

🌿 Watch on YouTube:
https://www.youtube.com/@RootedandThrivingMama

🌿 Follow on Instagram:
https://www.instagram.com/rooted.and.thriving.mama/


If you're looking for a Christian podcast about motherhood, Christian parenting, anxiety, postpartum depression, mom guilt, homeschooling, faith, biblical encouragement, and finding peace in Christ, welcome to The Rooted Mother. I'm so grateful you're here.

title: Ten Minute Medic
author: Dr. Bill Young, NRP, Ed. D., CP-C
description:
The Ten Minute Medic transforms difficult EMS concepts into practical clinical knowledge in just ten minutes. Whether you're preparing for the NREMT, succeeding in paramedic school, or becoming a better clinician, each episode explains the why behind physiology, pharmacology, cardiology, trauma, and medical emergencies. Expect evidence-based teaching, memorable analogies, a little humor, and practical lessons that help you think critically when every second counts.

BUT WAIT...THERE'S MORE!  It's absolutely, positively FREE!

title: Audio Face
author: Caleb Johnson
description: Audio Face is a podcast with all things story. Here you will enjoy podcasts breaking down popular books and how they compare to their movie adaptations, as well as my own version of audio books/ sleep stories.

title: The Parenting Pause
author: Naina Sharma
description:
Every Indian parent knows the feeling, your doctor says no sugar, your relatives say they turned out fine, and school homework is somehow on an iPad. Modern parenting is noisy, contradictory, and exhausting. The Parenting Pause exists to cut through that noise.

Hosted by Naina, a working mother and finance consultant, this podcast brings together experts across mental health, nutrition, education, sports, and Ayurveda, for real conversations that help urban Indian parents pause, breathe, and navigate it together.


🎧 Subscribe for new episodes biweekly.

Meet Naina. 👋
A finance consultant at the World Bank. Mother of two Gen Alpha kids. And now, the host of The Parenting Pause. By day, Naina helps companies navigate financial decisions. At home, she’s navigating something far more unpredictable: raising children in an age of screens, social media, AI, and information overload.

Like many parents today, she’s constantly learning, questioning, adapting, and figuring things out along the way. Because parenting doesn’t come with a manual, and every stage brings a new set of questions.

The Parenting Pause was born from those conversations. The ones parents have with themselves, with experts, and with each other as they try to make sense of modern parenting.
If you've ever wondered whether you're doing enough, making the right choices, or simply trying your best while figuring it out as you go, you're in good company.
Welcome to The Parenting Pause. ❤️

title: Pacific Nut Producer Live
author: Pacific Nut Producer
description: Pacific Nut Producer Live Podcast is the home for all of Pacific Nut Producer Live's content. It is designed to help you improve your tree nut growing practices and help your family or corporate farms become more efficient and resilient through changing market circumstances. For over 30 years, Pacific Nut Producer Magazine has served the western tree nut industry. This Podcast brings its content to a whole new level!

title: Instituto tranqui de la amistad
author: Esto no es radio
description: Explorando cómo se aborda la amistad en series, películas y la cultura pop y cómo todo eso se refleja en nuestras vidas

title: Ask Ms. Robyn
author: Robyn S. Hadley
description: Ask Ms. Robyn is the essential guide for parents and mentors navigating the transition of teenagers from high school to career.  Host Robyn S. Hadley interviews experts and families, offering practical advice on everything from securing financial aid, understanding the importance of GPA and transcripts to building a strong support team to get that first internship, apprenticeship or job!

title: Whisker Wisdom
author: St. Sophia's Forgotten Felines
description: Whisker Wisdom is the official podcast of St. Sophia's Forgotten Felines, where cat lovers, adopters, fosters, volunteers, and animal welfare professionals come together to explore the world of feline care and rescue. Each episode features expert insights, inspiring rescue stories, practical advice, and behind-the-scenes conversations about behavior, health, shelter medicine, and adoption. Whether you're caring for one cat or helping save thousands, you'll find knowledge, inspiration, and a community dedicated to giving every cat the life they deserve.

title: Marketing After Hours
author: Story Mark Studios
description: Being overwhelmed and overworked doesn't have to be the standard for marketing professionals. Marketing After Hours helps you stand out in an ever-changing market by providing proven tactics from industry experts. Hosted by Story Mark Studios Founder Richie Burke, you'll receive actionable strategies to help you grow your brand, generate leads, increase conversions, and build better relationships with your audience.

title: Isthmus Forum
author: GGMM Productions
description: Welcome to the Isthmus Forum podcast. The Isthmus Forum offers thoughtful, straightforward investment insights discussed in a roundtable format with various members of the Isthmus Partners team.

title: Prost! with the Leinenkugel's
author: Leinenkugel’s in partnership with GGMM Productions
description: Prost! with the Leinenkugel's is not a podcast necessarily just about beer or just about Leinenkugel's, but a podcast from a great beer family about everything that's going on in little Chippewa Falls, Wisconsin at the Jacob Leinenkugel Brewing Company, along with conversations with friends of the brewery; such as the likes of Christian Yelich, Eric Stoke, etc, and what we're doing in the great beer business. So we invite you to grab your favorite Leinenkugel beer, crack the top, whether that's a bottle or a can pour it straight down the middle, and start listening to Prost! with the Leinenkugel's.

title: Uhh... FIA
author: Fia
description:
Hello there my name is FIA. uhh… Fia is a raw and reflective podcast following the story of a 24-year-old Native American navigating mental health, identity, healing, and survival. Through honest conversations and personal storytelling, the podcast explores the lifelong impact of anxiety, depression, trauma, cultural expectations, family history, and the search for belonging.

Rooted in lived experience, each episode sheds light on the challenges of growing up while carrying emotional pain, generational wounds, and the pressure to stay strong. The podcast also highlights resilience, cultural pride, community, self-discovery, and the ongoing journey toward healing.

This series creates space for vulnerability, truth, and connection while bringing attention to mental health experiences often left unspoken in Native communities.

title: Siblings Secrets
author: Kora Bowman
description: This is a fun just talk podcast by Kora and Cole

title: The Holy Bible by GlpcJdb
author: Jonathan Britt
description: GlpcJdb studying the Holy Bible.

title: Autonomy of Blackness
author: Joi Divine Smith
description: The Autonomy of Blackness is an honest space for reflection, healing, and new learning. Hosted by Joi Smith, this podcast blends real-life storytelling with biopsychology, science, and spirituality to explore identity, emotional growth, and generational trauma—through the lens of a Black woman.

title: Scout Biscuit
author: Bucks Scout Radio
description:
The Scout Biscuit is a Bucks Scout Radio Podcast.
We started at the Bucks Scout Carnival Camp May 2026. See our seperate podcast for this "Bucks Scout Carnial Camp"
We talk Scouting - Its not just Badges. Also not just within Buckinghamshire, UK but wider.
If you want to be involved or respond to any episode, You can email studio@bucksscoutradio.co.uk

title: Soul Sessions with Cindy Canek
author: Cindy Canek
description:
A podcast by Cindy Canek 
Storytelling, transmissions, deep conversations, and channelled messages from something higher.
A space for your transformation, consciousness, and spiritual elevation.

title: BATL Podcast
author: BATL Podcast
description: The B.A.T.L. Podcast (Belief, Action, Truth, Life) is a Christian podcast dedicated to helping believers and seekers grow in their faith through verse-by-verse studies of Scripture and real-life conversations. Join Jesse, James, Preston, and Mark as they walk through books of the Bible, explore biblical truth, answer tough theological questions, and occasionally sit down with special guests to hear powerful testimonies and insights. Our goal is simple: know God's Word, live it out, and fight the daily battle of faith.

title: The Legislative Lens Podcast with Host: Rob Leatherwood
author: VCCAR
description: The Legislative Lens Podcast, produced by the Ventura County Coastal Association of REALTORS®, explores the housing crisis in Ventura County. VCCAR Director of Government Affairs Rob Leatherwood interviews top decision-makers and policy influencers as they analyze the causes of and solutions to our housing issues. With a healthy mix of levity and reverence, each episode tells the story of the intersection of government/politics and housing/real estate in a digestible and actionable way.

title: She CEO
author: Dr. Julie Ducharme
description: She CEO with Dr. Julie Ducharme, features inspiring conversations with women leaders, CEOs, entrepreneurs, and changemakers who are redefining success. Join Dr. Julie as she shares leadership insights, powerful stories, and strategies to help women elevate their influence, grow their businesses, and lead with purpose.

title: Venture Christian Church Weekend Messages
author: Venture Christian Church
description: Venture Christian Church is a non-denominational bible church in the San Jose area, led by Senior Pastor Tim Lundy. Venture exists to be a catalyst to transform the Bay Area for Christ.

title: Send It Podcast
author: Niclas Genovese and Eko33
description:
Discover the intersection of art, emerging tech and culture. Our podcast gives creators a voice, celebrating their freedom and innovation.
Join us for conversations on digital evolution and the creative world shaping our future.

title: Between Worlds
author: Ashley Ray
description: Between Worlds is a podcast about becoming, hosted by Ashley Ray. Born from the frequency of Jardín de los Sueños in Todos Santos, each episode explores the threshold between who we were and who we're becoming, through conversations with healers and guides who hold sacred space because they've crossed their own.

title: The History Matters Podcast
author: Knowledge Matters
description: Curious. Confident. Knowledgeable about the world. A content-rich approach to teaching history supports all this and more—even in our youngest students. Yet history has all but disappeared from American elementary schools. On the the History Matters Podcast, we explore the vast untapped potential of high-quality history instruction to build knowledge, accelerate literacy, and prepare students to participate in civic life. In inspiring conversations with curriculum experts, teachers, and instructional leaders doing this work in classrooms today, this podcast explores how history can animate the minds of young people and transform literacy, all at the same time.

title: Parenting Tools
author: Nicole
description: This is a podcast about raising kids, told from both sides of the table: a mom who's been through it all and her son sitting right across from her. No experts talking down to you, no perfect answers, just an honest, easygoing conversation between two people who actually lived it, together.

title: The Bootloader
author: Paul Cutler and Tod Kurt
description: A monthly podcast loading you up with news, project updates and product talk from the CircuitPython and maker communities. Hosted by Tod Kurt and Paul Cutler.

title: Harvest Bridge
author: Harvest Bridge
description: Harvest Bridge connects local ministry leaders in South Asia with people like you, here in the United States. Through our podcast we want to provide a place where you can hear their voices and listen to their stories in their own words. There is a transcription available for those who prefer to read these stories.

title: The Leading Edge
author: Commerce & Contract Management Institute
description:
The Leading Edge is a new thought leadership series, offering in-depth insights into the key challenges, innovations, and evolving dynamics of the contract and commerce landscape.


We’re bringing these insights to our community in a new way – through a curated series of articles and accompanying podcasts featuring expert voices and industry leaders.

title: Cents & Sensibility
author: Jeremy Clark
description: Entrepreneurial success doesn't exist in a vacuum. In Cents & Sensibility, host Jeremy Clark - CEO of CH Financial, but also a stand-up comedian - has candid chats with inspiring guests about how different factors intersect, interact, and interfere with the pursuit of personal, business, and lifestyle success. Join him for bite-size advice and real-life stories as he bridges the gap between education, humour, and modern business acumen.

title: Anyway… Here We Are
author: Kelsey Schiferl
description: This is my open diary to share unexpected stories and what God is teaching me through it all. If you want to laugh, cry, and be completely surprised by what's next, come along!

title: The Cindy Noir Show
author: Cindy Noir
description: Can We Talk About It?! Welcome to The Cindy Noir Show, your one stop shop for all things, Controversy, Culture, and Celf. Sit back, relax, and enjoy the show!

title: Podcoasters
author: Jack Connor
description:
Hello and welcome to Podcoasters! This is a podcast for fans of everything AFC Fylde and the National League presented, edited and produced by Fylde fans. We may not be the most professional or the slickest at this but we're giving it our best go and that's all you can really ask for. Let us know your thoughts.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Leading Healthcare AI
author: Temitayo Bewaji
description: Leading Healthcare AI explores how healthcare organizations can select, govern and implement AI responsibly. Each episode covers strategy, operating models, workforce readiness, clinical and operational use cases, risk, measurement and the practical work required to move from pilots to sustained adoption.

title: Elumi-Nation
author: Mark S. Hard LCSW
description: This is a show that cares about you what you think and what you’re going through. We’ll talk about mental health, faith, and just about anything else that interests you.

title: Women Who Give
author: Alex McCall
description:
Ever wondered if women give to philanthropy? Do women give differently, and if so, how do they like to give to charity? If you have asked these questions, this podcast is for you!

The reality is that the face of philanthropy is changing. Yet, many charities and institutions are still only raising funds from a small, non-diverse population of philanthropists. Women Who Give are on a mission to dismantle old perceptions about who is a philanthropist and how different groups give. We will speak with charity professionals, philanthropists, and philanthropy experts to better understand the women’s philanthropy landscape in the UK and beyond.

title: Church at the Grove - Walnut Grove Campus
author: Church at the Grove
description: Church at the Grove exist to reach people far from God and leading them to become followers of Jesus

title: The CircuitPython Show
author: Paul Cutler
description: An independent podcast with the people in and around CircuitPython. Created and hosted by Paul Cutler.

title: Smart Bear Live
author: Jason Cohen
description:
Jason Cohen ("A Smart Bear" online) and often a rotating bad-ass co-host get brutally honest with founders of real companies, who have real puzzles and struggles.

Like business therapy, we cut to the heart of issues, then build back up with ideas, options, strategies, and decisions.

title: She Thrives 101
author: Jalen Ventrone
description:
She Thrives 101 with Jalen Ventrone is a podcast dedicated to the women building businesses, creating impact, and thriving in their everyday lives.
Hosted by Raleigh entrepreneur Jalen, each episode highlights powerful conversations with female founders, creatives, and community leaders who are navigating the real journey of entrepreneurship, wellness, & personal growth.
From behind-the-scenes business lessons to honest conversations about building a life you love. 
Because thriving isn’t just about success.It’s about alignment, courage, and building a life that feels as good as it looks.

title: Pod Wars
author: Pod Wars
description: A deeper dive into the JBP universe.

title: The Proximity Advantage
author: Mark Timm
description: Podcast about family and business balance through the Power of Proximity Advantage.

title: Tom Talks
author: Tom Renney
description:
Hockey is more than a game. It's leadership, resilience, culture, and character.


Hosted by legendary coach and hockey executive Tom Renney, Tom Talks brings you conversations with some of the most respected athletes, coaches, executives, and leaders in hockey.


Drawing from decades of experience in the NHL, international competition, and leadership roles across the sport, Tom explores the lessons, stories, and defining moments that shape careers on and off the ice.

From player development and high-performance culture to leadership and the future of the game, Tom Talks delivers insights for players, coaches, parents, executives, and passionate hockey fans alike.


Join us for honest conversations with the people moving hockey forward.

title: Flourishing in Faith with Hannah Gillick
author: Hannah Gillick
description: Empowering godly women to flourish in faith, triumph in truth, and burn with His Spirit!

title: Coming Clean About Caregiving
author: Julie Peck
description:
Coming Clean About Caregiving is the raw, honest, and unfiltered guide to surviving life in the caregiving trenches. Hosted by veteran medical and business journalist Julie Peck (Medscape, Business Insider), this show strips away the polite clichés to talk about what caregiving is actually like.


Julie cared for her mother for six agonizing, beautiful, and overwhelming years until her death in 2022. Through that journey, she learned a lifetime of hard lessons—most of them too late to help. This podcast is the resource she wishes she’d had.


Each week, Julie brings together frontline family caregivers, top-tier clinicians, and elder care experts to share actionable advice, debunk hospice myths, and solve daily care logistics. From navigating medical data and building a care team to fighting caregiver burnout and handling the heavy weight of caregiver guilt, Coming Clean About Caregiving delivers the practical tools and validation you need to survive today.


We use a little humor, a lot of truth, and zero judgment to make the hardest job in the world just a little bit easier. Because you shouldn't have to figure it out alone.

title: Jam's Alley
author: Soul Jam
description:
Jam’s Alley is a bi monthly one hour Amapiano DJ mix hosted by artist, producer, and DJ Soul Jam. Every episode takes listeners on a carefully curated musical journey featuring the hottest new releases, timeless anthems, hidden gems, and exclusive selections, all seamlessly blended into one unforgettable mix.

From soulful grooves to high energy dancefloor moments, Jam’s Alley captures the sound and culture of Amapiano while showcasing Soul Jam’s signature style behind the decks. Whether you’re driving, working, relaxing, or getting ready for a night out, tune in twice a month for your next soundtrack.

title: 🪽 The Love Of Wisdom 🪽
author: SHANTEL FOSTER
description:
My show is about investigating the fundamental reality of existence philosophy and more! 
What can you expect from my show is to live your life understanding the systematic study to explore the value and nature of the universe.

title: To Know Ends: Honest conversations about aging, illness, and dying well
author: Chesapeake Health Partners
description:
What does it mean to live well — all the way to the end? To Know Ends is a podcast about the conversations we avoid, the care we don't understand, and the people who show up anyway. Each episode features voices from inside and outside the world of hospice and palliative care — nurses, social workers, caregivers, artists, and people who have sat beside someone they loved as they died — speaking honestly about what aging, decline, and dying actually look like, and what becomes possible when we stop running from those realities.
The title means two things at once. To know ends is to understand what a closing looks like. It is also to know no ends — to discover that confronting mortality honestly doesn't shrink a life. It expands it.

title: On My Momma Couch
author: niajones0711
description: We clearly have some explaining to do. Last year, we started a joint stream and spoke with y’all once a week about various topics! It was so fun, entertaining, and we were building a beautiful community of followers! Then one day, without warning, we stopped. In this episode we’ll talk a little bit about what happened and touch on the tough dynamics of mother/daughter relationships within the black community.

title: What's Your Favorite Scary Movie | The Cozy Horror Podcast
author: Scary Movie Pod
description:
*Pop your corn 🍿 light a candle 🕯️ double check your locks 🔑 and snuggle in for What’s Your Favorite Scary Movie?—the cozy horror podcast that feels like movie night with your best friends.*
Hosted by journalist and horror fanatic *Parker Herren* alongside former scary-skeptics turned genre-lovers *Caroline* and *Ian Hoyt* , we’re on a continuous quest to rank, review, and discuss cinema’s best (and worst) spooky flicks. What started 100 episodes ago as an attempt to convert two scaredy-cats evolved into a cozy, chaotic hangout space for horror fans of all levels.
From brand-new theatrical releases and streaming gems to iconic slasher classics, we break down scares, rank memorable kills, and put releases head-to-head for the title of our favorite scary movie, plus expert film history deep-dives from guest historian *Julieann Stipidis*.
🩸 *Join the Hangout:* Want us to read your review live on the show? Leave us a rating on Apple Podcasts!
📲 *Follow Along:* Catch full video episodes, vote on upcoming movie reviews, and check out the official leaderboard on YouTube, Instagram & TikTok: *@scarymoviepod*

title: Hospitality Unpacked Podcast
author: Noah Abel
description: Welcome to ’Hospitality Unpacked,’ where we explore the dynamic and diverse world of hospitality. Hosted by Noah Abel, a 16-year-old entrepreneur with a passion for the industry and an eye for innovation, this podcast offers you an insider’s tour through the tales and secrets behind the service. From interviews with seasoned hoteliers to chats with local cafe owners, ’Hospitality Unpacked’ serves up the stories that define hospitality culture. Each episode promises fresh insights into the workings of the industry, direct from the professionals who make it thrive. Tune in and join Noah as he unravels what truly makes hospitality a realm of endless discovery and opportunity.

title: marisa
author: marisa elaine
description:
Hey, I’m Marisa! Welcome to marisa—a cozy space for us to hang out every week and chat about pop culture, nostalgia, and personal growth. My life is a little bit of everything, so our episodes rotate too:
Wk 1: Books vs. movie/show adaptations.
Wk 2: Taylor Swift & music deep dives.
Wk 3: Reading my old journals & life experiences.
Wk 4: Manifestation & self-love.
Whether you're on a morning walk or relaxing at home, this feels like catching up with a friend. Grab a drink, hit follow, and let’s go on this journey together.

title: What Laura Likes
author: Laura
description: As wives and mothers, we find our path to Heaven through our vocation. What Laura Likes embraces the tasks of a Catholic wife and mother, seeking to help you work out your salvation in the home. Topics include our relationship with God, our husbands, our children, ourselves, the home, homeschooling, and meals. I pray that the content helps you to find joy in your vocation and to seek God in all moments, from cleaning the toilet to teaching a math lesson, and everything in between. Let us strive to become like Mother Mary in all moments of our pilgrimage here. Pax Christi,Laura

title: First Smoke of The Day
author: Blackleaf & PackGods
description:
Welcome to First Smoke of the Day – The Ultimate Cannabis Industry Podcast!


First Smoke of the Day is your go-to podcast for Exclusive insider Access to Cannabis Culture, Cultivation, Entrepreneurship, and more. Tune in for behind-the-scenes interviews with Cultivators, Hashmakers, Breeders, Brand owners, Athletes, Musicians, Smugglers, and the Fearless Hustlers shaping the cannabis industry. Discover Untold stories, expert tips, and valuable lessons from cannabis legends and innovators. From navigating underground movements to uncovering entrepreneurial success stories, we bring you real stories, hard truths, and actionable insights from the people driving cannabis culture forward.


Why Subscribe?


 •   Hear Stories from Industry Disruptors & Legends

 •   Learn From Experts Shaping the Cannabis Space

 •   Dive Deep into the Passion, Hustle & Culture


Subscribe now and join the First Smoke of the Day community!


Instagram

YouTube

BlackLeaf IG

PackGods IG

Website


Thank you so much for tuning into First Smoke of The Day. A cannabis and culture podcast giving you insight into the mind and lives of growers, extractors, playmakers, street brands, and underground culture in the global cannabis industry. Be sure to subscribe to the show on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you consume podcasts. Make sure to also leave a 5-Star review on Apple Podcasts and follow us on Instagram.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Sussex Preview
author: Trevor Knell
description:
Giving the world of Sussex non-league football the latest preview with some measured reaction, courtesy of Trevor Knell and the Sussex Stat Man Chris Prior.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The NEW YOU Pretty Podcast
author: NEW YOU
description:
Welcome to the pretty podcast, the signature podcast from pretty — a NEW YOU brand redefining beauty from the inside out. This is where we get real about all things pretty: menopause, libido, hormones, hair loss, hair growth, gut health, confidence, aging, and every unfiltered topic women actually want answers to.

Grounded in pretty’s beauty‑first supplement philosophy, each episode delivers honest conversations and practical solutions designed to help women feel balanced, vibrant, and unapologetically themselves.

pretty conversations. pretty wellness. pretty results.
Hit play and join the pretty movement — powered by NEW YOU. 💗✨

title: Food & Beverage Industry News
author: Prime Creative Media
description:
Food & Beverage Industry News is an Australian media publication covering the latest news and stories for the food and beverage manufacturing sector. 
https://www.foodmag.com.au/ 
https://au.linkedin.com/showcase/food-&-beverage-industry-news/

title: Salty Talks
author: Coach Jason Saltmarsh
description: Science-based training for high school distance coaches, from the coach behind 31 state champions. Salty Talks delivers short, no-fluff episodes on periodization, athlete development, race strategy, and the methods behind championship cross country and track programs. Hosted by Jason Saltmarsh, USATF Level 2 Endurance Coach and 2018 NH Cross Country Coach of the Year, drawing on 25 years of coaching and the training philosophies of Lydiard, Daniels, Vigil, and the Norwegian Method. One insight per episode you can use at practice on Monday.

title: Stocked & Loaded
author: Matt Sheen & Kyle Rogers
description: Talking everything from business, life & sales related to the Australian supply chain industry.

title: The Chapter After Podcast
author: Taylor Rush | The Chapter After
description:
The Chapter After is for the women rebuilding after survival.

Hosted by Taylor Rush, this podcast is a place for honest conversations about motherhood, trauma, marriage, boundaries, faith, grief, healing, and everything that comes after the hardest chapters of life.

No perfect endings. No pretending to have it all together.

Just real stories, real growth, and the reminder that you don't have to love the chapter to love the story.

Because there's always a chapter after.

title: KOKE Morning Show
author: KOKE FM
description: KOKE FM is the past, present, and future of Texas Country Radio! Download the KOKE app or listen live at kokefm.com

title: The Gym AND Therapy Podcast
author: Daryl Perry
description: The Gym AND Therapy Podcast, hosted by Daryl Perry of Your Level Fitness, explores the mental and emotional side of weight loss and living an active lifestyle. This solo podcast focuses on the softer side of growth, from mindset and self talk to emotional awareness and daily habits. Learn how to build a stronger relationship with yourself so your results are not just physical, but sustainable for life.

title: Admission Accomplished
author: VTAdmissions
description: Welcome to Admission Accomplished, Virginia Tech’s Undergraduate Admissions podcast! We’re checking off every step of the college journey, from applications and campus life to graduation. Whether you're exploring colleges and majors, or chasing your dream school, we're here to help you get that admission accomplished.

title: PREACH: A Poetry Podcast With Shantrell Genese Williams
author: Shantrell Genese Williams
description: God's word is her text and poetry is her platform. Join author Shantrell Genese Williams as she uses the art of poetry to paint a picture of Biblical truth. This is PREACHING like you've never heard it before!

title: Liberty Crack News & Special Information
author: Tripp Dettmering
description: This is an area for General News or Special Information with a Libertarian Slant.  We also feature specific LP Announcements and are open to post from around the country.

title: CastNet
author: Mahendra Gossai, Kevin Rain, and Ryan Sahadeo
description: Lifestyle podcast about topics in our life that appeals to us. Movies, TV, West-Indian culture, Food, and more.

title: Self-Produced
author: Oxana Vysochinskaya
description:
Can you build a creative career with no agency, no label, no budget? This podcast says yes — and proves it.
Every episode: one true story of an artist who produced themselves, taken apart by a producer. What actually worked, what would work today, and one concrete action to take this week.
Hosted by Oxana — producer with 15 years across TV, brand campaigns, exhibitions and photography projects.
New episodes weekly. Guides & consultations: selfproducer.pro

title: Jim Cantelon Today
author: Jim Cantelon
description: Jim Cantelon Today is an engaging verse-by-verse study of the Gospels (Matthew, Mark, Luke/Acts, John). Cantelon’s perspective is unique in that he planted the “King of Kings” Church in Israel and lived in Jerusalem with his family for seven years. He knows the country, culture, and history of Israel intimately, and brings those insights to his teaching.

title: Podcast Valley Sunday
author: Chris Paco and Jef Cameron
description: Hey Hey, we're The Monkees podcast! Well, WE'RE not the Monkees but we're two very big fans of the band/tv show/movies, etc! In Podcast Valley Sunday your hosts Paco and Jef break down each of the episodes of the TV series as well as rank every Monkees song into a huge list...and how do we do that, you may be asking? At the end of each episode of the podcast, we pull three songs out of a (green, wool) hat and add them in where we feel they belong. Will you agree with all the placements? No way! But you will love the banter and behind the scenes chatter that P&J bring to the table!

title: Shay and Garrett vs. Evil
author: Shay and Garrett vs. Evil
description:
A conversation about all things horror in movies, video games, books and much more! Join Shay and Garrett for a comedic look into the horror genre and everything it has to offer to send goosebumps up our spines; for better and for worse!
Stay groovy - and hail to the Pod, baby.

title: F8 Wellness with Dr. Ben Galyardt
author: Ben
description:
Your doctor said your labs are normal. But you still don’t feel right. We’ll get to the root cause, even when other practitioners have stopped looking.


Dr. Ben Galyardt is a functional medicine doctor and founder of F8 Wellness. He has worked with more than 15,000 patients over 25 years, most of them women who had been to multiple doctors and left with no answers.


This Podcast covers thyroid dysfunction, Hashimoto's, low ferritin, POTS, autoimmune root causes, postpartum crashes, and the bloodwork your doctor is not running.


F8 Wellness is a fully remote practice. Patients across the US can work with Dr. Ben's team directly.


New episodes every week. If you have been dismissed, this Podcast was built for you.

title: Remagin Eye Care with Dr. Raja
author: Deepak Raja
description:
Blurry vision. Burning eyes. Dropping your phone to zoom in because the screen keeps going fuzzy. 


If any of that sounds familiar, you're in the right place. Dr. Deepak Raja is a board-certified cataract and corneal specialist in Orlando, FL. 


This channel covers what most eye doctors don't have time to explain: why your dry eyes aren't getting better with drops, what actually goes wrong before cataract surgery, which lens is right for your life, and how to stop managing symptoms and start fixing the real problem.


New episodes weekly on dry eyes, cataracts, lens options, and the decisions your eye doctor should be walking you through, but probably isn't.

title: Hip Preservation with Dr. Almasri
author: Mahmoud
description:
Dr. Mahmoud Almasri is an orthopedic surgeon specializing in hip preservation, dual board certified in Canada and the United States, and a Fellow of ISHA, the International Society of Hip Arthroscopy. He has worked with over 15,000 hip patients and performed more than 2,000 surgeries, the majority of them hip-related.


This Podcast covers the questions hip patients are not getting clear answers to in their appointments: when a labral tear actually needs surgery, what happens to your hip while you wait, how to know if you're a real surgical candidate, and what separates the patients who recover well from the ones who don't.


Topics include hip labral tears, femoroacetabular impingement (FAI), hip arthroscopy, hip cartilage damage, diagnostic testing, recovery timelines, and hip preservation for athletes, especially soccer players.


New episodes every week.

After decades of dealing with mental and physical health issues, having low self worth, and being surrounded by toxicity, I decided to heal myself for myself. I spent years on my personal development journey, taking the holistic health route. I’m here to share relatable stories, insight and all of the knowledge I’ve gained on this incredible journey. Through reflective conversations, guest interviews, and thought-provoking prompts, each episode dives into topics like becoming the woman your old self would be intimidated by, navigating healing while managing everyday life, and embracing your highest feminine archetype.

This free podcast started as a vacation research project to organize my notes, but I realized I'd rather have a space to connect with other people like me, who are navigating this new AI world without a map. This isn't a salesy podcast—it's just my experience navigating AI, and I won't hide the bloopers and mistakes along the way. Yes, I'm very technical, but I also have no idea what to expect next since AI is constantly changing.
If you're like me, join my bandwagon. What's the old saying? Beam me up, Scotty!

Join the Police & Crime Commissioner as she puts your questions directly to the Chief Constable for South Wales Police. Together they discuss the issues impacting communities, the action being taken to tackle them, and the challenges facing modern policing.

Perfect for car rides, road trips, bedtime, classrooms, homeschooling, and screen free family time, The Kid Who Did What? gives kids ages 6+ exciting stories they'll actually ask to hear again, and gives parents educational entertainment they can feel great about.

author: Mars Initiative

*Every journey to Mars begins with a story.*
Mission Voices is The Mars Initiative's original interview series and podcast exploring the people shaping humanity's future in space.
Hosted by *Lexie Garrett* , each episode features conversations with astronauts, engineers, scientists, educators, advocates, entrepreneurs, and innovators working to advance the next era of human exploration. Together, we explore the stories behind the missions, the breakthroughs behind the headlines, and the ideas driving humanity's return to the Moon, journey to Mars, and future beyond Earth.
Whether you're a lifelong space enthusiast, a student dreaming of a career in STEM, or simply curious about where humanity is headed next, Mission Voices invites you to discover the voices inspiring tomorrow's explorers.

author: Skip Loehr
description:
Queer Kinship : a show about friends is a podcast exploring chosen family, the attachment dynamics of friendship, relational healing, friendship as activism, distributed co-regulation, and the many ways queer // queer-allied communities create, repair, navigate, and nurture belonging together. 
Skip is a licensed mental health counselor currently living on unceded Tewa land in Santa Fe, New Mexico. 
----
The conversations on this podcast are educational and reflective in nature. They are not a substitute for psychotherapy, medical care, or individualized mental health treatment. The views expressed by guests are their own and do not necessarily reflect the views of the host. Some episodes include discussions of grief, trauma, mental health, discrimination, conflict, and other emotionally difficult experiences.

title: Prisoners that change the world
author: Frank Stedman
description:
Prisoners Who Changed the World

Presented by Frank Stedman


What happens when someone is locked away from the world… and comes out having changed it?


In this podcast, Frank Stedman explores the lives of some of history’s most influential figures who spent time in prison — not just who they were, but how prison shaped their thinking, their mindset, and ultimately their impact on the world.


From political leaders to writers, revolutionaries to thinkers, each episode looks beyond the headlines to understand what prison does to a person — and how, in some cases, it can transform them.


This isn’t about glorifying crime.

It’s about understanding change, resilience, and the human mind under pressure.

Wherever you are in your walk today, the goal is the same: take the next step forward, grounded in Scripture and living on mission.

Every week, Say What? investigates the curious origins of the words, phrases, names and legends we use without a second thought. From Peeping Tom to Bob’s your uncle and everything in between, each short case file follows the evidence to uncover what really happened in less than fifteen minutes.

With interviews, dialogues and the occasional monologue, we explore our humanity through travel, the technology that enables it, and ecosystems that encourage elastic thinking and innovation.

title: Digital Dust: The Man Will Talk
author: BurnerSphere
description: The virtual Burning Man world featured in HBO's The Man Will Burn now has its own podcast about the doc. Four burners gather around a table inside BurnerSphere (BRCvr presents BurnerSphere) — the VR playa where doc star Lindsay Handren's Burning Man story began — to break down the series episode by episode: the culture, the history, and what HBO got right (and wrong) about the burn. We don't speak for HBO or the Burning Man Project — just burners with opinions.

description: The podcast that takes you inside the world of mediation and conflict resolution. We speak with leading mediators, conflict coaches, psychologists, researchers and industry experts to unpack the complexity and nature of disputes, look at what actually works when stakes are high and tensions run deep, understand what it’s like for the mediator, and to learn more about how people react when you bring them face to face to find common ground.

description:
A monthly podcast where one film from a physical media collection is pulled from the shelf to take a closer look at what makes it worth shelf space.
From the film itself, to the artwork, the packaging, and the special features—this is about celebrating movies the physical way.
If you’re a collector, a film fan, or someone who still loves opening a case and pressing play, this show is for you.

description:
Four guys. One crucible. No script.
What happens when you strip away the polished sermons and the rehearsed talking points? You get Refining Fire, a podcast where faith meets the heat of the moment. In every episode, four friends sit around the table with no idea what’s coming. The topic of the day stays locked away until it’s pulled live from the Crucible. What follows is raw, unscripted, and honest conversation about the Bible, life, and what it actually looks like to walk out your faith in real-time.
