author: Rony Galdamez
description:
Hosted by Rony Galdamez, The YTH Culture Podcast is a youth culture show produced by Pio Youth, the youth movement of the Shrine of St. Padre Pio Catholic Church in San Antonio.
This podcast highlights real stories from within our community and beyond it, with testimonies of how God is moving, conversations about culture, sports, and what’s happening in the world today, all through the lens of faith.
Each episode features honest conversations with guests, practical insights, and moments that point to God working in everyday life.
Faith, culture, and real stories.

title: Next Unicorn: The Next Great Tech Startups & Founders
author: Sasha Earnheart-Gold
description: Searching for the next great tech startups, we interview startup founders, venture capital investors, growth hackers, product specialists, technologists, legal experts, and others. Website: https://www.nextunicornpodcast.com/

title: This Is Lucy Podcast
author: Lucy Shahjahan
description:
This Is Lucy Podcast is a storytelling podcast about what happens when you stop betraying yourself, take the lid off who you really are, and start saying yes to the life that is calling you.
Through hilarious, vulnerable, and sometimes completely unbelievable stories from her own life, Lucy shares the wake-up calls, sliding-door moments, manifestations, romantic disasters, bold decisions, and divine interventions that helped her become the unapologetic woman she is today.
Expect conversations about love, self-trust, relationships, manifestation, healing, purpose, and following your heart even when it makes absolutely no sense on paper. 
This is not a podcast about pretending life is perfect or whispering affirmations while doing nothing. It is about doing the inner work, taking the risk, raising your standards, and becoming the version of you who is ready to receive what she actually desires.
Funny, fiery, honest, and deeply personal, This is Lucy Podcast is your weekly permission slip to stop playing small, trust your own wisdom, and go all in on your life.

title: Johan Speak
author: Charles Roberts
description: Hola, Mi nombre es Johan, pero me pueden llamar ¡SPEAK! No sabia que hacer con mi vida, empecé a hacer una voz y pues aquí estoy.   Hablo del CRINGE, porque yo también doy delicioso CRINGE :D

title: Usama and Dylan Save Your Life
author: Usama and Dylan Save Your Life
description:
Comedians Usama Siddiquee (@usamastandsup) and Dylan Palladino (@dylanjpalladino) make your life better. 
Send in advice questions and hinge profiles to wesaveyourlifepod@gmail.com

title: Introduction
author: Mia
description: Little short introduction

title: Philosophy's Not Boring
author: Kidopoly
description:
A groundbreaking podcast for curious kids aged 4-12, and the grown-ups listening with them, that proves philosophy, psychology and religion are anything but dull.

Join our fictional AI hosts Mira, a brilliant 9-year-old, and her younger brother Finn, age 7, as they explore philosophy, psychology and religion as one big family of questions about how we make sense of things. Each episode takes a real thinker or idea seriously, from what makes you the same person you were as a baby to why people across the world believe such different things about the soul, and then lands it somewhere in your actual life.

We don't hand you the answer and move on. Some questions have one; some have several worth comparing; some nobody has ever settled, and we'll tell you which is which. On matters of faith we stay even-handed: we describe what people believe from the outside, and never tell you what to believe yourself. Because philosophy isn't a pile of hard words to memorize. It's learning to ask a better question, and to sit with it honestly, even when it's uncomfortable.

A note on why we use AI. For us, AI allows us to deliver learning at a scale and quality that previously would have been too expensive. If we make the odd technical error, or the sound goes a bit funny, bear with us, we're trying our best. We hope you enjoy the show!

title: Perishable Crowns
author: Dez Ouzts & Noah Szilagyi
description:
Perishable Crowns is a sports podcast that examines athletics through a biblical worldview. We celebrate excellence, discipline, competition, and achievement while remembering that no championship, trophy, contract, or legacy lasts forever. Sports are wonderful gifts from God, but they make terrible gods.

Each week we discuss the biggest stories in sports, especially basketball, while exploring the character, integrity, virtues, failures, and worldviews that shape athletes, coaches, and organizations. We seek to appreciate greatness without idolizing it and critique failures without forgetting our shared human condition.

Our goal is to provide thoughtful sports analysis, fresh perspectives, and biblical wisdom in a media environment often dominated by outrage, tribalism, and celebrity worship.

title: SkyPlotter
author: Victoria Amelia Langley
description:
SkyPlotter is a deeply fascinating
scientific, and sensory-rich journey into the history of stellar mapping, astronomical technology, and the lost art of finding our way. If you are ready to quiet the digital noise, lift your eyes back to the night sky, and learn how to map your own path using the timeless laws of the universe.

title: Felt
author: Dana Biechonski Fernandes
description: Felt is a podcast about the body, emotions, healing, and the deeper intelligence moving through human experience. Hosted by Dana Biechonski Fernandes, somatic psychotherapist and educator, it explores embodiment, trauma, symptoms, relationships, and the wisdom of the body. Drawing from science, clinical practice, and lived experience, Felt explores what becomes possible when we slow down enough to truly listen. Dana translates complex ideas into conversations that are both grounded and deeply human—inviting a deeper relationship with ourselves, with others, and with life itself.

title: No Boss Required
author: Grant Reighard
description: Building a life without a boss one project, one decision, and one shipped product at a time.

title: The Technology Principal
author: Franklin Fitch
description: Dive into unfiltered conversations with the world’s top C‑level tech leaders as they share the bold moves, pivotal moments, and breakthrough ideas that shaped their journey. Each episode uncovers the challenges driving innovation today and the visions set to redefine tomorrow’s tech landscape. Inspiring, insightful, and packed with real leadership wisdom - this is where the future of technology gets personal.

title: Call Me Collect
author: Whuz & Earth1
description: Call Me Collect is the podcast where collectibles are just the beginning. Whether it's sports cards, Pokémon, comics, action figures, Bearbricks, Funko, vintage toys, or the latest hobby news and Pop Culture

title: Paradise Falls Podcast
author: Dear M D
description: Paradise Falls Podcast brings you immersive audiobooks and inspiring stories designed to entertain, uplift, and spark your imagination. Tune in and discover a world of meaningful storytelling—one episode at a time.

title: Hidden Stories of Tennis
author: Ethan walker
description:
Tennis is more than trophies, rankings, and championship points—it's a sport built on innovation, resilience, strategy, and unforgettable moments that often go unnoticed. Beyond the Baseline: Hidden Stories of Tennis takes you beyond the match highlights to uncover the fascinating stories, groundbreaking tactics, scientific discoveries, historic rivalries, and untold journeys that have shaped the game for generations.

Each episode blends documentary-style storytelling with expert insights, exploring everything from the evolution of the tennis racket and the psychology of elite performance to iconic tournaments, legendary champions, rising stars, and the hidden details that casual fans rarely hear. Whether we're revisiting pivotal moments in tennis history or explaining the biomechanics behind the perfect serve, every episode is designed to educate, entertain, and inspire.

title: Succesvol Transformeren Podcast
author: Transformation Tribes Highberg
description: In deze podcastreeks gaan consultants van Highberg met elkaar én met gastsprekers in gesprek over het belang van besturing en over succes- en faalfactorenten tijde van een Digitale Transformatie. In de snel veranderende digitale wereld van vandaag is het voor bedrijven en organisaties essentieel om zich aan te passen en te evolueren. Het is daarbij belangrijk om de juiste koers te bepalen en te bewaken voor en tijdens de veranderingen.

title: Beautifully Messed Up
author: Raven and Amethyst
description: A dark unfiltered podcast where chaos meets curiosity. Hosted with sharp wit and a touch of wicked humor, it dives into the raw, uncomfortable and often taboo corners of life - stories of secrets, survival, and relationships, and everything in between. It is a space where nothing is sugarcoated, vulnerability is power and the mess is what makes it beautiful.

title: Creative Juices Podcast
author: Breakfast of Champions
description:
Creative Juices Podcast is where creativity, branding, and sports collide.Hosted by Keegan Hartman, Assistant Director of Graphic Design at West Virginia Athletics, the Creative Juices Podcast takes listeners behind the scenes of the collegiate creative industry through conversations with some of the most talented designers, photographers, videographers, creative directors, and digital storytellers in sports.

Each episode explores the creative process, career journeys, branding philosophies, recruiting strategies, NIL, social media, content creation, and the ever-evolving landscape of athletics and visual storytelling. Whether you're a student looking to break into the industry, a seasoned creative searching for inspiration, or simply someone fascinated by the work that powers today's biggest athletic brands, this podcast offers authentic conversations and valuable insights from the people shaping the future of sports creative.

New episodes feature industry professionals from every level of athletics, sharing the successes, challenges, and lessons that have defined their careers.

Subscribe now and join the conversation as we celebrate the creators behind the brands, the stories behind the designs, and the passion that fuels the next generation of sports creatives.

Creative Juices Podcast — By Creatives, For Creatives.

title: TheTuesdayJewTX Podcast
author: phillip goldstein
description: Welcome to the ”Tuesday Jew” podcast—your spiritual wake-up call in a world full of distractions. We dive deep into Mussar, Chassidus, and Machshava, drawing on powerful teachings from Navardok, Slobodka, Telshe, Berdichev, Chabad, Breslov, Belz, Reb Elimelech, and Rav Kook. Tune in for a heimish perspective on navigating the madness of Ikvesa D’Meshicha and rising above the noise of modern technology.

title: Soul Sessions: Motivation for Introverts and People Pleasers
author: Benjamin The Great
description:
Soul Sessions with Benjamin The Great is a podcast dedicated to real conversations that inspire, encourage, and uplift. Each episode explores life, purpose, faith, relationships, personal growth, music, and culture with honesty and heart. Join Benjamin The Great for meaningful discussions, inspiring stories, and practical wisdom that speaks to the soul and empowers you to live with purpose. Each episode is a reminder that your quiet nature is not a weakness—it’s a strength. You don’t have to change who you are to succeed, make an impact, or live with purpose.
Whether you’re an introvert, a recovering people pleaser, or simply looking for motivation that speaks to the soul, this podcast is your space to grow, reflect, and become the best version of yourself.

title: The Path I Walk
author: Sydney Taylor
description:
What path are you on right now? Is it bumpy or smooth? Whichever path we walk on in our faith, God is constantly with us and he provides us with friends and family that walk the same path we do. Whether they are further ahead, behind, or right beside us. 
the.path.i.walk08@gmail.com

title: The Well-Trained Child
author: Eliza Southwell
description: I’m a Christian wife raising two boys. My strategies change. But Biblical principles don’t. So here's a picture of how children can be trained well. I tell stories from my home and cover topics like our routines, how I train my children, language I use (and avoid) and how motherhood is an opportunity to improve my character too.

title: In The Dog Pound
author: Kevin Scoop Culp
description: An inside look at Camden High School Bulldog sports, including interviews with coaches, players, alumni, etc.

title: Jeremy's Podcast
author: Jeremy
description:
Belief Beyond the Streets Podcast

Welcome to Belief Beyond the Streets, a podcast where hope rises above hardship and every story has the power to inspire change. Hosted by Jeremy Blaine, this podcast explores faith, redemption, personal growth, prison reform, second chances, and the resilience of the human spirit.

Through honest conversations with formerly incarcerated individuals, community leaders, advocates, and changemakers, we uncover practical tools for healing, accountability, leadership, and breaking generational cycles. Whether you’re seeking inspiration, navigating life’s challenges, or passionate about creating stronger communities, this podcast is for you.

Because true transformation begins with belief—and your past does not have to define your future.

title: For the Greater Good
author: Diana Olloova
description: Shining a light on the people behind local businesses. Honest conversations about the highs, the lows, the dreams, the goals, and everything in between.

title: Estate Expectations
author: Elisabeth Pickle
description:
Are you ready to transform your perception of Estate Planning? What if I told you, it's not just about paperwork, but a journey towards securing your legacy and turbocharging your brand? Intrigued? You should be! Say goodbye to confusion and hello to clarity with the Estate Expectations podcast. I'm Elisabeth Pickle, your guide in this adventure. With two decades as an estate planning attorney, I've demystified the process, safeguarded fortunes, and paved futures. 
Get ready to plunge into the depths of estate planning, unravel the threads of legacy creation, unravel the secrets of wealth transfer, and shield assets like never before. We're not stopping there – intellectual property protection, brand building, and much more are all on the menu.
Your assets, both tangible and intangible, deserve a fortress of protection. Let's empower your journey together. Tune in on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or wherever podcast waves carry you.  Your wealth-securing, brand-boosting, legacy-crafting voyage starts now!

title: Condé Nast Traveller Podcast
author: Condé Nast Traveller
description: Escape Routes with Condé Nast Traveller is our new podcast showcasing the magazine's most transportative travel storytelling. Let us take you to some of our favourite places, if only in your imagination, with the soul-lifting memoirs and daydream-worthy destinations of our most-loved travel stories, read aloud by the writers who penned them.

title: Midweek Service - RCW Phoenix
author: Jason McNeill
description: Midweek services for the Phoenix Restored Church Worldwide

title: farley philosophy bts

description: Behind the scenes of my philosophy work, think of it as a silence removed thinking lived stream.

title: Cardinal Corner
author: Leighton Media Podcast Network
description: Coaches and student-athletes sit down on the Cardinal Corner to discuss the changing atmosphere of division three athletics and their journeys to becoming involved on Saint Mary's athletic teams.

title: ASRÉ PODCAST
author: Arlington Scott
description: GOD | FAMILY | FELLOWSHIP. My life has been filled with lessons that I'm still learning from to this day. I now have blessings that God has given me to pass the lessons on to. This society has pulled us out of connection and makes us think our personal stories don't matter anymore, but that's just not true at all. Join me as I present a life of perseverance through ups and downs in my life and conversations with some very accomplished guests. This will be the realest podcast you will ever listen to because its your life reflected in others. Lets Fellowship.

title: The Badass Rules Podcast
author: Jules Duncan
description: Talking all things health & fitness

title: Based onna true story
author: brissa garcia
description:
Hey guys, what's up! I'm an artist from South Central LA. Follow me on ig too know what i look like @bvxx77 This podcast is the product of me finally having the balls to put myself out there. From unhealed childhood wounds, debilitating anxiety, making all my friends hate me, and an unconsensual rehab hold in Mexico, to getting healthy, speaking my truth, and learning to be a person again, this is my podcast, raw and uncut. Sometimes it might be funny; other times it may be uncomfortable to listen to. The only thing that I promise is authenticity. I've spent enough time hiding and being ashamed! Either way, I hope you join along for the ride. This goes out to anyone who might feel alone. Sometimes, even though you can't imagine a different reality, it doesn't mean there isn't one


Sincerely, Briss

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Chronicles of the Un-napped and the Walking Fed
author: Chris and Tiffany Gonzales
description:
Most people never realize that fostering is one of the most emotionally demanding and spiritually rewarding journeys you can take. Chris and Tiffany Gonzales share how they stepped into foster care—and ended up impacting over 13 lives in just two years. They reveal the raw truths behind the highs, the heartbreaks, and the miraculous moments of their foster family story.
Discover how listening to their story might reshape your understanding of true sacrifice and God's calling.

title: The Bounce Back Podcast
author: The Carruth Center at WVU
description:
The Bounce Back Podcast is a podcast for students at West Virginia University that breaks down resilience into real, usable tools. Join clinicians Laith Alabbad and Spring Lepack as we discuss building resiliency through honest conversations, campus-relevant stories, and clinician insight that doesn’t feel clinical. This podcast is presented by WVU’s Carruth Center’s Resiliency Project.

This podcast shares general mental health information, but it’s not a substitute for therapy. If you are a current WVU student interested in starting therapy, reach out to the Carruth Center for Counseling and Psychological Services at 304-293-4431 or schedule an appointment online at carruth.wvu.edu.

title: May She Reign
author: DALYA
description:
May She Reign is a podcast about the people who build lives on their own terms.

Hosted by Farida Raafat, founder of custom clothing brand DALYA, each episode explores the risks, pivots, instincts, and defining moments that shape who we become. Through candid conversations with founders, athletes, creatives, leaders, and change makers, May She Reign uncovers what it really means to trust yourself, carve your own path, and reign in your own life.

title: The invincible brain
author: Dr. Majid Fotuhi
description:
Your brain can get better at any age, and Dr. Majid Fotuhi will show you how.
A Harvard and Johns Hopkins-trained neurologist and author of the USA Today bestseller The Invincible Brain, Dr. Fotuhi turns cutting-edge neuroscience into simple steps you can act on this week.
Each episode explores the five pillars of brain health, exercise, diet, sleep, stress reduction, and brain training to help you sharpen memory, beat brain fog, and build a stronger, more resilient brain.
New episodes every week.
Take your free brain health assessment at invincible.drfotuhi.com

title: From love
author: ssofiaseni
description: Sofia Seni is a 21 year old girl starting her spiritual/ self growth journey. She has created a modern day wellness podcast to share her learnings and help others grow with love.

title: עד לבית משלכם עם אלירן חרץ
author: Eliran Haratz
description:
.אלירן חרץ משתף בכל מה שצריך לדעת כדי לקנות בית בישראל. מלמד את סודות המימון, התהליכים מאחורי הקלעים של השקעות נדל”ן שמקדמות את המשפחה מבחינה כלכלית. התוצאה – בית משלכם ודירות להשקעה
פודקאסט על כלכלה, משכנתאות, מימון וצמיחה כלכלית.

title: Elephants on Business
author: Elephant Advisory
description: Elephant Advisory is a prominent accounting and broking firm based in Melbourne, with a successful history of growing Australian businesses. This podcast will feature Elephant team members breaking down every unique challenge of small business ownership in Australia, from the good times, the bad times and the ugly times. With a little help, you'll be prepared to make the right decisions for your business and avoid making our many mistakes. So join us as we begin a podcast journey to improving your small business. www.elephantadvisory.com.au/services/accounting

title: Do It or Don’t
author: Big Time Publishing
description:
Conversations with women who finally did the thing.
Started the business. Wrote the book. Shared the art. Learned the hobby. Changed directions. Became visible. Bet on themselves.
A show about creative courage, unfinished dreams, identity, motherhood, ambition, and the moments that change everything.
Because whether you do it or don’t… time keeps passing.

title: Between The Pours
author: Dancing Water Winery
description: Between The Pours is New Zealand's rosé podcast, long-form conversations with the winemakers, viticulturists, and passionate people behind the country's finest drops. Hosted by Kim Schofield of Dancing Water in Waipara, North Canterbury, each episode goes beyond the tasting notes and into the real stories: the harvests that went wrong, the risks that paid off, and the moments that made someone choose a life in wine. New episodes every month. Subscribe and join us between the pours.

title: The Future State
author: Dr Emmanuel Ogungbemi
description: The Future State explores how technology, artificial intelligence, law, leadership, governance, and society are shaping the world of tomorrow. Hosted by Dr Emmanuel Ogungbemi, each episode helps leaders, professionals, policymakers, and curious minds understand what is changing, why it matters, and what we should do about it.

title: In The Mind of JT Podcast
author: beardedguyonaharley
description:
Step into the unapologetic world of a Marine, biker, musician, and patriot who’s loud, proud, and unfiltered. Expect bold opinions, raw truth, and a few laughs along the way. Whether you’re here for the wisdom, the wild stories, or just to hear someone who lives life full throttle, you’re in the right place.

👉 Buckle up, ride free, and don’t take any sh*t from anyone.

title: Selah with Say
author: Selah with Say
description: Selah with Say is a Christ-centered space for guided Christian meditation, breathwork, prayer, mindfulness and scripture reflection. These gentle, Scripture-rooted meditations help overwhelmed Christians to slow down, breathe, receive God’s Word and rise with peace, healing and spiritual strength. Rooted in Jesus Christ, the Word of God and the presence of the Holy Spirit. Rest in God. Rise in strength.

title: The Wednesday Society
author: The Wednesday Society
description: A podcast that takes a philosophical deep dive into forgotten or intentionally neglected texts.

title: Radiological Technology News
author: radiation
description:
A news podcast that keeps you up to date with the latest developments in radiology and medical imaging.
From medical AI and diagnostic imaging to conferences, events, and the latest research, we break down the topics that matter in a concise and easy-to-follow format.
Designed for busy healthcare professionals, it's a convenient way to stay informed during your commute or whenever you have a few spare minutes.

title: Injecting Leadership
author: Injecting Leadership
description:
Injecting Leadership is the podcast that discusses leadership through the lens of the medical aesthetics industry.
Hosted by two seasoned leaders from the medical aesthetics industry, each show features a discussion with a special guest where they’ll explore how the precision, care, and perspective needed in medical aesthetics translate into powerful leadership lessons.
Each episode delivers inspiring stories and practical tools for motivating teams, driving innovation, and leading with love.

title: Watch Design Time
author: Sean
description: Welcome to Watch Design Time, a podcast dedicated to the art, engineering, and creative philosophies behind watch design and watchmaking. We move past the superficial noise to focus on how timepieces actually come to life. From independent microbrand creators working through physical prototyping constraints to the design teams inside historic houses like Bulgari, we explore the real stories behind the wrist. Join us as we break down the distinct cultural influences, mechanical limitations, and design choices that shape the watches we love.

title: Where You Meet Yourself
author: Liv Arcangeli
description:
Somewhere beneath the roles you play and the expectations you carry, there's a version of you waiting to be set free. Welcome to Where You Meet Yourself.
I'm Liv Arcangeli — transformational coach, energy healer, and artist. I'm here to get real with you. We strip away everything that is not you, so you can return to the core of who you truly are. Because from that place your magic lives, your boldness comes alive, and you create the work, the art, and the impact you're here to make.
This is your invitation to meet yourself, again and again, maybe for the very first time.

title: Everything is insurance
author: Liberate
description: The show that asks the questions about insurance nobody else thinks to ask, then goes and finds the answer. One honest question per episode, from supertankers to the World Cup, each one revealing a corner of the world that couldn't run without insurance. Everything is insurance. Even the things that aren't.

title: Uncle Marv's Podcast
author: Uncle Marv
description: Conversations with friends and almost famous people.

title: WrestleVision
author: ItsLukeMania
description: WrestleVision is a weekly wrestling podcast covering WWE through the eyes of a fan who rediscovered the industry after years away. From Raw, SmackDown and Premium Live Events, every episode offers honest opinions and thoughtful discussion - without the noise!

title: Where Are We Going? A COD Zombies Podcast
author: Shutter Paw
description: Join longtime Call of Duty Zombies fans Shutter Paw, Stanley557, and The Korborium as they explore the Dark Aether. From Nacht Der Untoten to Rex Infernus and every twisted chapter in between, no topic is off limits. Packed with passionate discussion, lethal analysis and the occasional bit of patter, this podcast is the perfect companion for slaying the undead hordes.

title: Grind Theory
author: Chris Madden
description:
Welcome to Grind Theory, the podcast for anyone who loves specialty coffee or wants to learn more.
Each episode I explore a coffee from roasters around the world, covering its origin, producer, processing, brewing, tasting notes, and the story behind the cup. I’ll taste each coffee as espresso and filter, sharing honest thoughts and what I learn along the way.
Grind Theory is also about the people, the culture, and making specialty coffee welcoming and accessible. So grab a coffee, get comfortable, and let’s explore together.

title: On The Gear
author: Infinity Motorcycles
description: Tom & Tom bring you their world of motorcycles and gear from inside the industry. A podcast powered by Infinity Motorcycles.

title: Judy's Movies
author: djjustconnor
description: Judy’s Movies, a new podcast series. Hosted by Judy Garland superfan Connor Hibbard, Judy’s Movies will explore the life and legacy of the world’s greatest entertainer through a close look at the movies she made.

title: Under Wraps with Jessica
author: Jessica Hyam
description:
Under Wraps with Jessica is a space for real stories with real people, meaningful conversations, and the journeys that shape us. From women sharing their personal experiences with head wrapping to the occasional unexpected turn, no two episodes are exactly alike.
Some stories are about what we wear and others are about who we become along the way. Either way, there’s always more to discover Under Wraps.
Hosted by Jessica Hyam from The Little Tichel Lady.

title: The Stakes-N-Eggs Show
author: Stakes
description: Where uneducated and educated opinions matter. Catch a quick scramble with a side of beef filled debates as Stakes & Guest from all walks of life re-evaluate this thing we call life with some of the most random topics and POV's.

title: The Book of Psalms Podcast: Scripture, Christian Prayer, Bible Readings, and Daily Devotion
author: Podily
description:
The Book of Psalms Podcast features peaceful Bible readings, Christian prayer, Scripture reflection, and daily devotion inspired by the Book of Psalms. Each episode shares biblical wisdom, encouragement, worship, hope, healing, and spiritual guidance to help listeners grow in faith and draw closer to God.

Listen for calming Psalm readings, Christian meditation, morning devotion, evening prayer, Bible study, and uplifting Scripture for everyday life. Whether you are seeking peace, strength, comfort, inspiration, or a deeper understanding of the Bible, The Book of Psalms Podcast offers meaningful Christian content rooted in God’s Word.

title: TheDrewskiPodcast
author: Drew
description: The Drewski Podcast is a Raw approach to the Experiences as a 21 Year old Entrepreneur

title: Hidden Battles
author: GodDesign, LLC
description:
Hey, everybody, I’m Anne-Marie Michaelis, a Christian Author and Coach. Welcome to the Hidden Battles Podcast! A weekly podcast about the hidden battles of spiritual warfare, the struggles in our minds and hearts, AND the truth about overcoming these “hidden battles.” My life was radically transformed when I was saved and delivered during a supernatural encounter with Jesus. God allowed my eyes to be spiritually opened long enough to SEE the truth of these hidden battles and to scare the rebellion against Him- right out of me. 
DISCLAIMER: visit www.goddesign.me/disclaimer

title: Signal Stitch
author: Signal Stitch
description:
Master the business of fashion with Signal Stitch.
We connect creative innovation with the realities of retail, supporting designers, label founders, retailers, and industry pros.
We dive into honest conversations exploring the journeys of UK fashion designers, brands, and retailers. 
From sustainable production to scaling retail operations, we deliver insights you need to navigate today’s fashion landscape.
Join the host and expert guests as they share practical knowledge and real-world experiences to help you build a lasting fashion business. Follow & visit signalstitch.co.uk to stay updated

title: Untangled
author: Sam Scibetta and Sushaant Bakhru
description: Asia's Untold Business Stories.

title: The Blueprint Grid (Silas Kingsley)
author: Silas Conrad Drake Kingsley
description: Hey everyone, Silas here!

title: Burnt Shores
author: Rick Devereaux | Caribbean and Sea Thrillers
description:
Cap Gilroy was just an aimless Uber driver scraping by on the Florida Gulf Coast when a marina night guard is brutally murdered on his late father’s classic boat. Hoping to just stay out of the way of the police, Cap discovers that his absentee dad wasn't just a paranoid survivalist, but a deep-cover CIA operative who hid a package before his death, a package everyone now seems desperate to find.
Now, the ultimate deadly treasure hunt is on. Pursued by cartel assassins and entangled with a lethal Mossad spy, Cap must team up with his father's formidable former CIA partner to find the hidden truth before their enemies do. 
Burnt Shores is a pulse-pounding Caribbean thriller where family legacies are forged in gunfire and the deadliest secrets are hidden at sea. Each 5-15 minute episode is a pulse-pounding sprint to a secret Cap may not want to find. 
A new chapter. Every day. For free. From Surface Tension Studio.

title: Nikki Cosma: Real Talk, No Filter
author: Nikki Cosma
description:
Unscripted & unapologetic conversations from a free-spirited, sex-positive, self-employed, festival-girlie travelling the world in the 21st century - I’m Nikki Cosma, facilitator and founder of Holofractal Life Systems & Retreats. 
No notes, no script, just what’s real. New episodes whenever I feel there’s something worth sharing with y’all. Expect my craziest story-times (I will have you cry-laughing at my best and worst moments!), reflections on emotional regulation, personal self-care philosophy, women’s health navigation, financial literacy development, living a life of flow and literally anything and everything else that pops into my cheeky lil’ brain.
Tune in, have some laughs and always remember - the magic within me is blessed to be witnessed by the magic within you!
Thanks for being here :D
— Nikki Cosma ＜3

title: Produced By
author: Second Signal Media
description: Go behind the scenes with the hosts, producers, editors, and creators who make podcasts happen. Explore everything that happens before, during, and after the recording, and discover what success really means for a podcast

title: Conscious Accomplishment
author: Scott Britton
description: A podcast with Scott Britton on how to balance the process of spiritual growth and professional success.

title: Zachary Studying for FCP Exam Help 2026

Pro surfer, best-selling author, and Soul Surfer Bethany Hamilton joins her husband Adam Dirks to explore how faith, family, and intentional living make all things possible—even in life’s toughest moments. From raising kids and homeschooling to health, discipleship, and navigating today’s culture, this podcast delivers real conversations and practical encouragement. With inspiring guests and powerful stories, All Things Possible is your weekly dose of hope, resilience, and purpose-filled living.

title: The Spirit
author: ADB
description:
The Spirit shares real stories of faith from people living in country Australia. It is for those already living with faith and those curious to know more about what that actually looks like. It is a glimpse into the lives of people living in country Australia and how being there has shaped their faith.

These are the stories of a police officer, an army cadet, a recipient of an Order of Australia and an advocate against family and domestic violence just to name a few. Their stories are not always neat, and each challenges us to think differently. But ultimately these are encouraging stories of hope.

The Spirit was recorded on the lands of the Dja Dja Wurrung, Baraba Baraba, Yorta Yorta and Bangerang peoples.

title: Disability Dialogues
author: Kimberly Walljasper
description:
Have you ever found yourself in a situation where you don’t know what to do or say because you don’t have any background knowledge or prior experiences?
Welcome to Disability Dialogues! This is a bi-weekly podcast that spreads awareness about individuals with disabilities. I am your host Kimberly Walljasper, and together we will explore inclusion through expert interviews as well as personal stories. Whether you have experience with individuals with disabilities or not, this podcast is here to provide you with resources so that you can be educated in this area and build meaningful connections.  
As a pre-service special education teacher with over eight years of experience working with various nonprofits like the Penguin Project, Spectrum Project, and Best Buddies, I’ve had the privilege of learning firsthand about the diverse challenges and triumphs faced by individuals with disabilities. From navigating the early days of volunteering to becoming deeply involved in advocacy, I’ve gained valuable insights into inclusion, adaptability, and understanding. This journey has taught me the importance of patience, perseverance, and building genuine connections. I understand the concerns you may have because I’ve walked that path myself, learning and growing alongside the incredible individuals I’ve had the honor to support.
Through my experience with individuals with disabilities, I discovered the incredible capabilities within every person, regardless of their challenges. This realization changed how I view inclusion, teaching me that everyone has a unique voice and story that deserves to be heard. This led me to start my podcast, where I can spotlight those stories and amplify voices often overlooked. Now, I want to share these powerful insights, personal triumphs, and practical guidance with you, helping to foster understanding and a more inclusive world for all.
So, if you’re ready to make a change in the community, subscribe to Disability Dialogues.

title: The Redox Podcast
author: Mark Porteous and Russell Mariani
description:
Health begins with communication. Every cell in the body relies on signals that support recovery, resilience, vitality, and healthy aging, yet cellular communication remains one of the most overlooked aspects of wellness.
Hosted by Russell Mariani and Mark Porteous, The Redox Podcast explores the science of redox signaling and why it matters through conversations with scientists, clinicians, educators, entrepreneurs, and wellness leaders. Complex science is translated into practical, accessible conversations that connect cellular health with everyday life, helping make sense of how the body communicates, adapts, and supports its own natural processes.
The ASEA Redox technology is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease or medical condition. The statements made during this podcast have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. People featured in this podcast may have received remuneration through the ASEA compensation plan. Most consumers of the ASEA redox products use them as part of an already healthy lifestyle. Results may vary. This information is intended for the US market only.

Host Brandon Straka, founder of the #WalkAway Campaign, sits down with some of the most fascinating and often controversial voices in the country. title: 1025 Black Sheep The memoirs of me
author: JAMAL DOCTOR
description: For Jamal, growing up on 147 street "grandmas house" wasn't entirely bad but it wasn't good either. learning what drugs and entitlement can do first hand to a family1025 BLACKSHEEP' he shares his life story.Born and raised in South Jamaica Queens, Jamal Doctor lived a life of revolving emotions. This in his words is a soul baring tale of how he realized the hard truth of being the “Black Sheep” of the family. The obstacles he faced and the lack of support from his family and how much harder life was because of it. Learning to navigate family relationships while always being the one in the wrong was taxing mentally and physically. Being loyal and loving to a family that never loved him became his cross to bear.

Mormon to Muse is a podcast for women navigating life after the Mormon church — the faith transition grief nobody prepares you for, the identity loss that comes with leaving a high-control religious system, and the slow, honest work of reclaiming yourself.
Each week I explore what it means to find yourself again after the Mormon church — through creative practice, life coaching tools, and the kind of honest conversation you can't have at Sunday dinner
If you've spent years being everything for everyone else and you're only now starting to wonder who you actually are — you're in exactly the right place.

title: A Loving Disruption with Elena Kunkel
author: Elena Kunkel
description:
A Loving Disruption starts with a claim I can’t leave alone: the opposite of depression isn’t happiness. It’s expression. 

By expression, I mean having access to all of yourself - your appetite, anger, humor, ideas, desire, taste, conviction - and letting those things have consequences. They change what you make, what you say, what you choose, what you refuse, how you love, and what you contribute.

I’m interested in the distance between functioning and feeling fully alive. How can someone be wildly capable and still feel like she’s managing her life from behind glass? What cracks it wide open? Why can making something honest bring you back into your body? Why do we become vague around the things we mean most? And what happens when fear has been holding the pen for so long that we mistake its voice for our own?

Each week, I follow those questions with people who have spent their lives approaching them from different directions: artists, founders, neuroscientists, somatic practitioners, coaches, philosophers, astrologers, Human Design people, and others who know something about what brings a person online. And what shuts her down.

They have different language for the same strange gap. I want to hear where their answers touch. Where they contradict each other. What each one can see that the others can’t.

This is the experiment: externally processing and testing the idea messily here, in my own life and with the sharpest people I can get in the room, until the language becomes specific enough to reach you when you feel far from yourself—and useful enough to help you find your way back.

title: Confidently Crypto (and More)
author: Dr. Tonya Evans
description:
Dr. Tonya breaks down cryptocurrency, blockchain, and decentralized finance (DeFi), current regulations and laws into simple, actionable insights — helping you separate fact from fiction, break barriers, and take control of your financial future. Whether you're crypto-curious, skeptical, or ready to take the plunge, this podcast is your go-to resource to navigate the exciting world of digital finance—and more—with confidence!
Dr. Evans, an attorney and former law professor, has testified before Congress, advises central bank crypto policy in West Africa, and she sits on the board of the Digital Currency Group. She has been in the rooms where it happens. She doesn’t hand you “crypto bro” hype. She delivers clarity and then gives you the tools to handle what will come next.

In each episode, we'll talk with pastors, financial leaders, SBC ministry leaders, and the team at The Baptist Foundation of Alabama about the practical side of ministry finance and its Kingdom impact. Generally, our conversations will be framed through our service offerings, such as legacy giving, investments, ministry accounting, generous giving, vision campaigns, church loans, scholarships, and the everyday stewardship decisions that shape the future.

 

"Now to him who is able to do far more abundantly than all that we ask or think..." (Ephesians 3:20)

Hosted by Kimberlee Jennette, each episode explores what happens when what worked before no longer fits—and why that doesn’t mean your best years are behind you. Through honest conversations, expert guests, and real stories, we’ll talk about health, purpose, relationships, work, aging, technology, faith, and what it means to keep becoming.
If you’ve ever looked around and wondered, Is this really how the story goes?—you’re in the right place.
Because reinvention has no expiration date.
